name: Lezgian
notes: Lezgian (лезги чӏал) of Dagestan and Azerbaijan, written in a Cyrillic alphabet. The grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Lezgian has a large consonant inventory with a four-way stop contrast (voiced / voiceless aspirated / voiceless unaspirated / ejective) and uvular and pharyngeal series, encoded by Cyrillic digraphs and the palochka ⟨ӏ⟩; establishing the letter/digraph → IPA table reliably requires a proper descriptive source (Haspelmath's grammar), which has not been consulted. Metadata only.
